requiredFindings.summary: The patch trusts x-middleware-subrequest from the client.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: An unauthenticated external request can set the same header and skip middleware protecting private routes.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Do not trust this header in app middleware; upgrade Next.js and strip/reject internal headers at the edge or reverse proxy.

requiredFindings.summary: The new test only proves the bypass branch exists.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: It does not assert that a public request with a spoofed internal header is rejected.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Add protected-route tests with spoofed x-middleware-subrequest and unauthenticated users.

optionalFindings.summary: Route handlers should not rely solely on middleware for high-value authorization checks.

disallowedConclusions: The patch is safe because the header is used by the framework.
Only checking the exact header value is enough.
